author: jtraglia
content: This is an interesting idea. Creating a thread.

author: kevaundray
content: <@554799849288105986> Are you thinking of making this configurable? cc <@402080271563751435> for visibility

author: kevaundray
content: Context here is that zk attesters may not have an EL

author: ethdreamer
content: It's just that right now, all CL clients already do this for forks >= Bellatrix

author: kevaundray
content: makes sense -- seems reasonable to have it as a default so that you don't store redundant data. If its not too much effort, would be great to have it be configurable

author: taulepton_
content: Yep, I noticed this when running integration tests for our zk attestor lighthouse fork. When RPC block requests are made against zk attestor nodes (with no EL), they fail to fulfil the request and are ultimately penalised. If you could make this configurable, that would be great.

author: ethdreamer
content: Wait do we no longer have a flag to disable this for previous forks?

author: taulepton_
content: Not sure, I didn't get a chance to check. I can take a look.

author: taulepton_
content: I hacked around it in the interim by syncing from non zk-attestor nodes in the first instance.

author: taulepton_
content:
Possibly this configuration in the store?
```
    /// Whether to prune payloads on initialization and finalization.
    pub prune_payloads: bool,
```

author: .paulharris
content: i think <@641548687813902336> is basically looking at this currently
